payer.name: UnitedHealthcare
payer.policy_title: Botulinum Toxins A and B – Commercial Medical Benefit Drug Policy

drug.drug_class: Botulinum Toxin Type A and B
drug.limitations_of_use: Daxxify (daxibotulinumtoxinA-lanm) is typically excluded from coverage. Coverage reviews may be in place if required by law or the benefit plan. Coverage for Dysport, Xeomin, Botox, Myobloc is contingent on criteria in the General Requirements and Diagnosis-Specific Requirements sections.

criteria.description: Botulinum toxin administration is no more frequent than every 12 weeks, regardless of diagnosis

One of the following: (a) Prescriber attests dosing is in accordance with the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved labeling; OR (b) For indications without FDA approved dosing, prescriber attests there is published clinical evidence to support the dosing
History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the following: Botox, Dysport, Xeomin
Initial authorization will be for no more than 12 months
Reauthorization will be for no more than 12 months
History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the following: Botox, Dysport
Initial authorization will be for no more than 12 months
Reauthorization will be for no more than 12 months
History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the following: Botox, Dysport, Xeomin
Initial authorization will be for no more than 12 months
Reauthorization will be for no more than 12 months
History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the following: Botox, Dysport, Xeomin
Initial authorization will be for no more than 12 months
Reauthorization will be for no more than 12 months

exclusions.description: Daxxify (daxibotulinumtoxinA-lanm) is typically excluded from coverage. Refer to the Medical Benefit Drug Policy titled Medical Benefit Therapeutic Equivalent Medications – Excluded Drugs and the corresponding excluded drug list with preferred alternatives.

Daxxify (daxibotulinumtoxinA) is typically excluded from coverage; coverage reviews may be in place if required by law or the benefit plan. Refer to the Medical Benefit Drug Policy titled Medical Benefit Therapeutic Equivalent Medications – Excluded Drugs and the corresponding excluded drug list with preferred alternatives.
Pelvic floor spasticity (and associated pain conditions) – removed from proven indications list as of 01/01/2026.
Stiff-person syndrome – removed from proven indications list as of 01/01/2026.

confidence_scores.review_flags: Document is truncated at page 2 of 28. Only the General Requirements (applicable to all indications) were captured. The Diagnosis-Specific Requirements section — which contains individual indication-level PA criteria — was not included in the provided text. Multiple covered indications and their specific criteria are missing. Full extraction requires the remaining 26 pages. Daxxify exclusion noted. Confidence in completeness is low due to document truncation.
